title: 'Coulter questions' — Sept 2020 counselor-meeting prep
html:
TL;DR

  • Five-line Apple Note from Sept 5, 2020, 3:58am — Yahir is 17, the first weeks of COVID era remote senior year at Grants Pass High. Title is "Coulter questions:" — agenda-list for a meeting with a counselor / advisor named Coulter.
  • Body strands: "Any in person help" (a COVID-pandemic-specific question — was in-person counseling available?), "Changing of classes," "AP classes 1 quarter," "Sourcing," "Bridge Quest."
  • Coulter is a new entity — a GPHS counselor / advisor / teacher whose name doesn't appear elsewhere in the indexed corpus yet. Surfaces here as the agenda-target for an academic-planning meeting; meeting purpose was Yahir's senior-year course planning during the chaos of pandemic remote schooling.
  • "AP classes 1 quarter" — explicit question about whether AP courses were offered for only one quarter that year (a COVID era schedule disruption). Anchors the pandemic-effect on Yahir's AP-stack (APUSH etc.).
  • "Bridge Quest" — likely a GPHS credit-recovery / bridge-program name (or a senior capstone variant); not corpus-explained. Queue for next mention.
  • Same-day companion to the late-night "Why and where are you going to transmute" manifesto note — Sept 5, 2020 is a same-day double-register entry: dawn logistics + midnight metaphysics.

Source

"Any in person help / Changing of classes / AP classes 1 quarter / Sourcing / Bridge Quest"

Why it matters in the graph

  • First corpus appearance of Coulter as a GPHS counselor figure. Stub-creation threshold met (proper-noun person Yahir scheduled a deliberate meeting with).
  • Anchors the Sept-2020 senior-year-during-COVID context for later notes — confirms the schedule was actively being negotiated, classes were being shuffled, and AP availability was unclear.
  • Demonstrates the same-day high/low double-note pattern that defines .

Open questions

  • Coulter — first name? Counselor, AP teacher, or college-prep advisor? GPHS staff in 2020?
  • Bridge Quest — credit-recovery program, capstone class, or scholarship?
  • "Sourcing" — sourcing what? College apps? Scholarships? Internships?

Wikilinks
